methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Naifo Technologies is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company founded by Gaëtan Séverac that has raised €54.7M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of nine same-sector companies at Series B through Series D stages, comprising eight private firms and one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ). Key private peers include 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), and Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given data availability. The nine-company peer median valuation stands at $1.6B, with a range spanning $300M (low) to $10.0B (high). This median reflects valuations of robotics companies at similar or adjacent funding stages and represents the most defensible anchor point for this analysis.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.4B (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $1.6B. This range is supported by the peer cohort but carries meaningful uncertainty given the wide dispersion in stage and outcomes across the peer set—notably the $10.0B valuation for 1X Technologies at an earlier stage. Key unknowns affecting valuation include Naifo's specific competitive positioning within the robotics sector, unit economics maturity, and revenue trajectory relative to the peer median.
